instruction: Head across the room towards the front door then turn left. Go up the stairs and turn right. Enter the bedroom then wait.

text: I'll start by observing my surroundings.
I'm looking around the living room to locate the front door, scanning past the fireplace, chairs, and windows to get my bearings.
I'm in a living room. I need to find the front door. Let me look around by turning.
I'm spotting a white French glass door on the left that might be the entrance, so I'll keep turning to get a full view of the space.
I can see a door on the left. Let me continue looking around to identify the front door.
I'm panning around the space and now seeing the kitchen area with its cabinets and glass-fronted storage.
This is a kitchen/pantry area. Let me continue turning to complete the survey.
I'm panning around the room to complete a full 360-degree view, taking in the dining setup with the table, chairs, mirror, and the doorway that opens into the adjacent space.
A dining room. Let me continue turning to complete the survey.

I'm back at the starting fireplace room, so I've completed a full loop. The white glass French door on the left side of the living room from my second observation is most likely the front door. Now I need to navigate back to that view and follow the instructions: head toward the front door, turn left, go up the stairs, turn right, enter the bedroom, and wait.

From my current orientation, I need to turn left 6 times to face that front door again.
I've completed a 360° survey. The front door (white glass-paned door) was visible earlier. Let me turn back to face it.
I can see the white glass-paned front door on the left side of the room, past the sofa. I need to turn left to face it directly and then move toward it.
I can see the front door (white glass-paned door) on the left. Let me turn slightly left to face it and move toward it.
I overshot to the left and now I'm looking at the kitchen cabinets instead. The white front door is actually on my right, so I need to turn back that direction to face it properly.
I turned too far; the front door is now on the right. Let me turn right to face it directly.
I'm noticing the white glass-paned door is positioned to my left at about 30 degrees, so I'll make a smaller turn of about 2 steps to center it in my view, then move forward toward it.
The front door is ahead-left. Let me turn left slightly and move toward it.

I'm orienting myself to the space—there's a pillar directly in front of me blocking the view, and the white glass-paned front door is positioned beyond the seating area to the right. I need to navigate around this obstruction to get a clearer path toward the entrance.
The front door is beyond the pillar. Let me turn right slightly to align with the open path and move forward.

I can see the white glass-paned front door more clearly now, positioned at the center-left past the pillar with a chair in front of it. There's an open path through the room, so I'm moving forward a few steps to get closer to the door.
The front door is visible past the pillar. Let me move forward across the room toward it.

I can see the white French doors at the front of the room and the staircase with its railing on the left side. Following the instructions, I need to head toward the front door first, then turn left to access the stairs.
Now I can clearly see the front door (white double doors) ahead, and stairs on the left. Let me move a bit closer to the door area, then I'll turn left toward the stairs.
I'm approaching the front door now, and I can see the stairs off to the left with their railing visible. Following the directions, I need to turn left toward the stairs to head up them.
I'm near the front door. Now let me turn left to find the stairs.

I can see the staircase now, positioned toward the upper left with its banister visible. After turning left, the stairs are ahead of me, but there's a sofa blocking the direct path to their base. I need to navigate around the furniture to reach the stairs, adjusting my position slightly to align with them.
I can see the staircase now. Let me align with the stairs. The base of the stairs appears center-left. Let me move forward toward it.

I'm orienting myself to the layout—the stairs climb up the left side toward the back, with a console table underneath, while the front door sits on the right. There's furniture blocking the foreground, so I need to navigate around it to reach the staircase.
The staircase is clearer now. I need to reach its base, which is toward the center-left. Let me navigate around the furniture toward the stairs.

I'm tracing the path to the stairs more carefully—they're positioned along the left wall, with the base situated behind the console table and mirror setup. The gray bench on the left is a separate piece blocking direct access, so I need to navigate around the console area to reach where the staircase actually begins.
The stairs base is toward the center. Let me move forward toward it, navigating around furniture.

The furniture is blocking my direct path forward. I can see the window seat along the left wall and the bench in front of me, with the staircase in the center. The dark wooden floor in the center-right looks clear, and there's a hallway passage to the left of the staircase that might give me a way through.

Since I'm stuck against the furniture, I should turn right to find open floor space and navigate around the obstruction.
I seem blocked by furniture. Let me turn right to find clearer floor space and approach the stairs from a different angle.

I'm orienting myself to the space—the front door is center-right, the staircase rises to the left, and there's a console table with mirror positioned near the stairs. The instruction is to head toward the front door then turn left to go up the stairs, so I'm mapping out where I need to move next.
Now I see the foyer clearly: front door center-right, stairs on the left. Let me move forward into the open floor, then head to the stairs base on the left.
The sofa in the foreground is blocking my path forward, so I need to navigate around it. I'm thinking about turning right toward the open door and foyer area to find a way past this furniture.
Still blocked by the sofa. Let me turn right toward the open foyer floor by the door and move there.

I'm oriented toward the front doors now with the stairs visible to my left. I can see the white rug in front of the entrance and the banister descending on the left side, which aligns with the directions to head to the front door then turn left toward the stairs.
I'm now facing the front door with stairs on my left. Let me turn left to face the stairs and find their base.

I'm trying to work out the spatial layout of the staircase. It appears to be a straight run ascending toward the upper left, with the bottom step positioned near the center of the image by the console table. There's a hallway passage on the left side where the stairs actually begin, though I'm still clarifying exactly where the base of the staircase sits in relation to the overall room geometry.
I'm facing the staircase which rises up-left. Let me move forward toward its base.

I'm running into a collision with the window bench on the left side of the room. The spatial layout is more complex than I initially thought—there's the bench, the console table under the stairs, and the sofa all competing for space, and I need to reconsider how the staircase actually sits in relation to these elements. The stairs seem to rise toward the back-left, with a hallway opening visible behind the furniture.

Let me try navigating around the right side instead. I'll turn toward the open dark wood floor on the right, move forward a few steps into the center of the foyer to get clear of the furniture, then reorient myself to approach the staircase base from a different angle where the lowest steps should be more accessible.
I'm colliding with the bench. Let me back up by turning right toward open floor and repositioning.

I'm orienting myself in this space again—the front door is center-right, stairs on the left, with dark wood flooring and a white rug in the middle. There's furniture blocking the way, particularly a sofa and ottoman in the foreground. The clearest route forward is along that central dark wood floor toward the door, but to reach the stairs I'd need to navigate left toward the banister near the console table. Let me look more carefully at the staircase...

The stairs actually ascend toward the upper-left, with the first step positioned where the banister is lowest—around the center-left near that console and mirror. There's a dark opening at the far left that seems to lead to a landing or passage. My best approach is to move forward onto the open floor between the sofa and the door, then angle left toward the stairs. The white rug is blocking my direct path, so I'll shift right slightly to skirt around the sofa and access that clear wooden floor near the entrance.
Let me reposition into the open foyer floor near the door, then approach the stairs. Moving toward the open dark floor.

I've moved into the foyer and the front door is now on my left. I need to reorient myself to figure out where the stairs are relative to my current position, since the instructions say to head toward the door and then turn left to go up the stairs.
I'm in the foyer with the front door on my left. Let me turn left to face the door directly.

I'm orienting myself in the space—the front doors are positioned center-right, the staircase is on the left side, and there's a console table with mirror toward the center-left. Following the instructions to head toward the front door then turn left up the stairs, I can see the staircase clearly now with its banister descending toward the lower-left corner.

Let me move forward into the open floor area and approach the base of the stairs on the left side.
Good clear view. Front door center-right, stairs on the left. Let me head toward the base of the stairs on the left.
I can see the staircase now—white steps at the lower-left with the banister on the left side and the front door to the right. I need to turn left to face the stairs and head up.
Now I can see the base of the staircase on the lower-left. Let me turn left to face up the stairs.

I'm at the base of the stairs now, looking up at them as they ascend toward the upper-left with a landing visible at the top. I need to move forward to start climbing, though I should watch out for furniture that might be in the way.
I'm now facing the staircase. Let me start climbing by moving forward up the steps.
I'm at the base of the stairs now, which curve upward to the left ahead of me. The steps rise directly in front, so I need to move forward and slightly left to align with their path as they ascend.
I'm at the base of the stairs. Let me climb up, following the steps which rise up-left.
I'm climbing the stairs now, moving upward as they curve left toward a landing. The steps continue ascending in that direction.
I'm on the stairs climbing up. Let me continue up.
I'm navigating up a staircase that curves or turns, likely making an L or U-shape. The flight I'm on leads to a landing, then the stairs continue upward to the left.
Continuing up the stairs which curve left. Let me keep climbing.
I'm making my way up a staircase that curves to the left, with greenery trailing along the banister as I continue climbing.
Still climbing. Let me continue up the stairs.
The staircase keeps winding upward with a landing where it curves toward the upper-left, so I'm continuing to climb along this ascending path.
The staircase curves up. Let me continue climbing, following the curve.
I've overshot to the left and hit a wall, so I need to pivot right toward the staircase and keep climbing upward.
The stairs continue up to my right. Let me turn right to face them and continue climbing.

I'm almost at the top of the stairs now, with a landing visible ahead and a doorway on the left that opens into a room with green furniture—likely the bedroom. I need to finish climbing these last steps, then turn right and enter that bedroom.
Near the top of the stairs. Let me climb the last steps to reach the top landing.

I've reached the landing at the top of the stairs and can see a bedroom doorway on the left with green bedding visible. The instructions say to turn right and enter the bedroom, but the bedroom appears to be on the left—I'm trying to reconcile the layout with what I'm being told to do.
I'm at the top of the stairs. I see a bedroom through the doorway on the left. But the instruction says turn right—let me survey the landing to understand the layout.

I've turned right into a hallway and can see a room with furniture and windows that could be the bedroom I'm looking for. There's a console table with a mirror ahead, and I need to determine if this room on my right matches the instruction to enter the bedroom after turning right at the top of the stairs.
Turned right. There's a room to my right with furniture and windows. Let me turn a bit more right to face that room.

I'm looking at the layout of this space - there's a bedroom visible through a doorway ahead and to the left, furnished with a chandelier and seating. I'm currently on the upper landing overlooking the foyer, with the staircase railing and front entrance visible to my right. I should head toward that doorway to enter the bedroom.
I can see a room with a chandelier through the doorway ahead-left—likely the bedroom. Let me head toward that doorway.

I can see the bedroom clearly now through the doorway on the right—there's a bed, pillows, a chandelier, and French doors visible. The instruction is to enter the bedroom and wait, so I need to turn right and move forward through that entrance.
I can clearly see the bedroom through the doorway on the right—bed, chandelier, windows. Let me turn right and enter it.

I'm positioned at the bedroom doorway with the bed visible ahead. There's a railing artifact blocking the direct path, so I need to adjust my angle slightly left to center on the doorway before moving through.
The bedroom doorway is straight ahead. Let me enter the bedroom by moving forward through the doorway.

I'm noticing the railing is blocking my path forward, so I need to navigate around it to reach the bedroom doorway that's now visible at center-right. Let me adjust my angle to face the doorway directly and move past this obstacle.
The bedroom doorway is at center-right. Let me turn right to face it directly and move through.

I'm looking at the same space again—the bedroom doorway is ahead at center-left, but there's a stair railing blocking my path forward. I think I might actually be positioned at the top of the stairs looking down at the stairwell, with the bedroom entrance across a small gap that the railing is preventing me from crossing.

The doorway keeps shifting in my view depending on my position—it was centered earlier, then I moved too far right, and now I'm back. The forward movements aren't getting me closer because the railing is in the way. I need to go around it by turning left and approaching from that side instead.
I'm blocked by the stair railing. The bedroom doorway is to my left. Let me turn left to approach the doorway from the side, avoiding the railing.
